MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-15-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Atty. NIKOLAUS W. REED, Esq. of Law Office of Nikolaus W. Reed (Tel: 415-940-7766) for Plaintiff Stacey Sorrells;
Atty. RIANA E. DANIEL, Esq. of CLAPP, MORONEY, VUCINICH, BEEMAN+SCHELEY (Tel: 925-734-0990) for Defendants Herrero Boldt, Hyde Park Management, LLC, and Sutter Bay Medical Foundation.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS SUTTER BAY MEDICAL FOUNDATION, HYDE PARK MANAGEMENT, LLC, HERREROBOLDT LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants Hyde Park Management, LLC, Herreroboldt, LLC, and Sutter Bay Medical Foundation's motion to strike punitive damages from the complaint is denied. Plaintiff alleges that she was the victim of an intentional assault and sexual battery by Yonas Girme and Feleke Gebregiorgis, whom she alleges were employed by the moving defendants, and that she had reported a similar prior incident by one of them to defendants, but they failed to take any action. Civil Code section 3294(b) provides, "An employer shall not be liable for [punitive] damages . . . , based upon acts of an employee of the employer, unless the employer had advance knowledge of the unfitness of the employee and employed him or her with a conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others or authorized or ratified the wrongful conduct for which the damages are awarded." Where, as here, a plaintiff alleges that an employer had advance knowledge of the unfitness of an employee and with a conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others continued to employ him, the employer may be held liable for punitive damages. (Weeks v. Baker & McKenzie (1998) 63 Cal.App.4th 1128, 1153-1160 [law firm properly held liable for punitive damages where it had advance knowledge that partner was likely to sexually harass employees and exhibited conscious disregard for the rights and safety of others by failing to take reasonable steps to prevent his misconduct].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041, email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs Complaint Ccp 436
Off Calendar Jul-09-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SUTTER BAY MEDICAL FOUNDATION, HYDE PARK MANAGEMENT, LLC, HERREROBOLDT LLC's Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs Complaint Ccp 436.

After the Court adopted the tentative ruling, Counsel informed the Court by email that the matter should be heard on 7/15/20 instead of 7/8/20 due to the unavailability of Plaintiff's Counsel. Therefore the matter is to be heard on July 15, 2020. Calendared in Error.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR# 6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, NOT Reported. =(302/EPS)
